title: stylidium lobuliflorum
intro: Stylidium lobuliflorum, also known as the trigger plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. It is native to Western Australia and is known for its unique ability to trigger a movement in its leaves when touched. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The trigger plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits including relief from colds and flu, as well as soothing sore throats and skin irritations.
